brief: Subscribers receive digest emails with duplicate notification entries when the same trigger is retried via SDK timeout.

root_cause.description: collectDigest in digest.step.ts queries the Redis list by subscriberId but does NOT deduplicate by transactionId. Retried triggers append new entries to the same list, producing duplicates that are later emitted in the digest payload.
root_cause.mechanism: Redis LRANGE returns all entries including duplicates from retried triggers; no Set-based dedup on transactionId

required_findings.description: Agent must identify the worker digest step/subsystem as the failure area
Agent must identify that deduplication by transactionId is missing from the collection step
Agent must identify the Redis list (vs Set) data structure as the mechanism that allows duplicate entries
Agent must identify digest.step.ts around lines 389-400 (collectDigest) as the exact location

anti_patterns.description: SDK timeout triggers the retry that exposes the bug, but the root cause is missing deduplication — not the timeout itself
The duplicate entries exist in the digest payload before dispatch; the email provider receives and faithfully sends what it is given
BullMQ job-level deduplication prevents the job from running twice; it does not deduplicate entries already appended to the Redis digest list by the first run

notes: A score-4 investigation will also note that a fix requires either switching the Redis list to a sorted set keyed by transactionId, or applying a distinct filter in collectDigest before building the payload. The retry path is in packages/worker/src/services/trigger-event.service.ts and is not itself defective.
